raw:
CONCORD - TOP PRIORITY

- create an ios/android front-end application that is capable of connecting to a concord server.

- It would be really nice if we could find a way to charge for the native phone interface. Everything else that you need to run the program is free and you can access fropm you rphone in a browser. I want to be able to sell the tiny convenience of having a native-feeling experience on their device.

- I also want to create a companion ios server application that can utilize a phone to host a call. The user would turn this on and then go over to the front-end application and interact. This turns their phone into a private comms relay that they can have rich chat interactions with people through.

- I need a whole suite of ntaive applications for all platforms. Server and Front-End. The server is always capable of serving a webpage so the front-end is completely optional and mostly cosmetic.
optimized:
### OPT-001: Native mobile client apps for Concord (iOS + Android)
Build native iOS and Android frontend applications that connect to a Concord server. Feature parity with the web client is not required — these apps are primarily a polished, native-feeling UX layer over the existing server API. The server's built-in web UI remains the canonical free interface; native clients are optional cosmetic alternatives.

Source: Bullet 1 + Bullet 4
Priority: high

### OPT-002: Monetization model for native frontend clients
Design and implement a paywall / purchase flow for the native mobile frontend apps (OPT-001). Everything required to run and use Concord must remain free via the browser-accessible web UI; the native apps are the sole paid tier, sold as a convenience/UX upgrade. Deliverables: pricing model proposal, store listing strategy (App Store / Play Store), and in-app purchase or paid-download integration.

Source: Bullet 2
Priority: high

### OPT-003: iOS companion server app (phone-as-relay)
Build a native iOS application that runs a Concord server instance on the phone, turning the device into a private comms relay. Users toggle the server on, then connect to it from the frontend client (OPT-001) to host calls and chat sessions routed through their own phone. Must support voice call hosting and full chat functionality. Consider: background execution limits, NAT traversal, battery impact, and discovery/pairing UX between the host phone and connecting clients.

Source: Bullet 3
Priority: high

### OPT-004: Full native app suite across all platforms (server + frontend)
Expand OPT-001 and OPT-003 into a complete native application suite covering all major platforms for both roles:
- Frontend clients: iOS, Android, macOS, Windows, Linux
- Server apps: iOS, Android, macOS, Windows, Linux (companion/relay servers)

The Concord server always serves a web UI, so native frontends remain optional. Deliverable: per-platform build targets, shared codebase strategy (e.g., Tauri/Flutter/React Native evaluation), and release pipeline for each store/distribution channel.

Source: Bullet 4
Priority: high
